preamble: Depalatalization of [ʃ] to [s] involves the movement of the articulation from the post-alveolar to the alveolar region.

examples.note: While most [ʃ] became [x] in Modern Spanish, in some dialects and loanwords, it shifted to [s].
Greek lacks [ʃ] and often adapts this sound from loanwords as [s].

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
